extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL attacked inside 1km when ROGLIC refused to share pace work after 9km Quick-Step tempo control, won by 2 seconds but lost GC on tiebreak
tactical_patterns: Soudal Quick-Step tempo from 9km isolated Roglic's UAE support, forcing him into unequal two-up negotiations without teammates.
Van Gils held quality breakaway threatening enough to prevent UAE from controlling pace, enabling Soudal's sustained tempo strategy.
Evenepoel's stage bonus seconds (4s) neutralized by winning margin under GC tiebreak threshold, revealing miscalculation in final celebration deceleration.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: controlled 9km tempo riding through team, then finished with acceleration when tactical negotiations failed
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: tactically correct refusing pace work while isolated, maintained GC lead through tiebreak despite being outnumbered
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: attacked without protecting UAE leader YATES when team cohesion needed against Soudal's numerical advantage
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: dropped on sustained tempo rather than explosive attacks, signals continued recovery limitations
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: refused to abandon breakaway cooperation despite GC proximity, forcing peloton's extended chase effort
rider_signals.VAN GILS Maxim: positive: last breakaway survivor into final 9km, quality enough to complicate UAE's pace calculations
